title: Oxford Dictionary Record (Schema v2)
description: Schema for a single word record produced by the ielts-deck Oxford parser. One record per (word) at the JSONL top level. Multi-POS is nested under pos_data.

word.description: Headword. Null only on synthetic records.

homonym_index.description: Oxford homonym index (1, 2, 3...) for words with distinct etymologies sharing the same spelling. Null for non-homonyms. Display as superscript: bass¹. The merge step groups by (word, homonym_index) — bass1 and bass2 are NOT merged.

source.description: Always 'oxford'. Two separate schemas ensure this invariant.

source_url.description: Oxford/Cambridge URL the record was scraped from. Null when generated from cache (caller fills in).

source_files.description: List of cache filenames that contributed to this record (for traceability).

pos.description: Top-level POS labels declared on the page (usually 1, can be multiple for multi-POS words).

register_tags.description: Top-level (word-wide) register tags. Always [] in v2 (no source exposes word-level register in the parsed HTML).

oxford_lists.description: Oxford 3000/5000 list membership. Empty for Cambridge (always []).

oxford_badge.description: Word-level CEFR badge from Oxford 3000/5000 top-of-page span (ox3ksym_* / ox5ksym_*). Distinct from per-sense def.cefr — this is Oxford's tier endorsement for the whole word.

uk_ipa.description: Headword UK IPA, e.g. '/ˈeɪbl/'. Extracted from the first .phon under .phons_br (Oxford's British parent block) at the top of the page. Wrapped in slashes already; do not re-wrap.

us_ipa.description: Headword US IPA, e.g. '/ˈeɪbl/'. Extracted from the first .phon under .phons_n_am (Oxford's North-American parent block) at the top of the page. Wrapped in slashes already; do not re-wrap.

opal.description: OPAL word list designation (e.g. 'OPAL spoken', 'OPAL written'). Oxford-only; not implemented in v2 (cross-ref phase).

awl.description: Academic Word List designation. Oxford-only; not implemented in v2 (cross-ref phase).

uk.description: UK pronunciation audio URL. Oxford: data-src-mp3 on .sound.audio_play_button. Cambridge: <source> child of <audio> with uk_pron in src.

us.description: US pronunciation audio URL. Same selector logic as uk.

see_also.description: Related headwords from SEE ALSO section. Oxford: span.xrefs + span.xh. Cambridge: .xref.

register_tags.description: POS-level register tags (rare; not implemented in v2)

sensenum_local.description: Oxford's literal sensenum attribute on <li class='sense'>. Nullable for idioms/phrasal.

countability.description: For noun senses: 'countable' ([C]), 'uncountable' ([U]), 'both' ([C, U]). Null for non-noun senses or when no countability marker is present.

domain.description: Academic subject domain extracted from span.labels (e.g. 'biology', 'law'). Null when no subject marker is present. Single value (first match wins for compounds).

cefr.description: Per-topic CEFR. Nullable: Oxford sometimes tags a topic without a level.

collocations.description: Buckets of collocations keyed by category (adverb / phrases / verb + head / etc.)

is_phrase.description: True if phrasal verb. Not implemented in v2 (always false).

is_idiom.description: True if idiom (detected via span.idm-g ancestor walk)

pos_data.description: One entry per POS section. Each contains all senses for that POS.

oneOf.description: Oxford only: parsed from <table class='verb_forms_table'>. All keys optional — Oxford exposes whatever forms it has. Core 5 (root, thirdps, past, pastpart, prespart) appear on regular verbs; 3 modal keys (neg, short, rareshortform) appear on modals only.

phrase.description: Idiom phrase as it appears on Oxford page

examples.description: Idiom examples (from ul.examples > li > span.x inside the idiom's sense block)

idioms.description: Top-level idiom phrases (extracted via span.idm-g for Oxford). Each entry: phrase + text + examples + cefr. Cambridge does not have this section.

_skip.description: Build-layer flag: when true, the Anki builder must skip this record (no extractable senses). Set by merge.py when both pos_data and idioms are empty.

_skip_reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why _skip was set (e.g. 'phrasal-verb-redirect: no extractable senses').
